name: Servo Motor
tagline: Precision angular positioning motor controllable from 0° to 180° via PWM signal
description: A servo motor is a rotary actuator that uses PWM signals to precisely control the shaft angle from 0° to 180°. The SG90 is a micro plastic-gear servo ideal for lightweight projects, while the MG995 is a metal-gear servo capable of higher torque. Servos are used for steering mechanisms, robotic arms, camera tilts, and any application requiring precise angular control.

pinout.name: VCC
pinout.desc: Power 4.8V–6V (use external supply for large servos)

pinout.name: Signal
pinout.desc: PWM control signal — connect to a PWM-capable Arduino pin

wiringNote: For multiple servos or MG995, use an external 5V power supply — not the Arduino 5V pin (max 500mA from USB). Share GND between the external supply and Arduino. The SG90 can be powered from Arduino's 5V for light loads.
commonMistakes: Powering MG995 from Arduino 5V pin → voltage drops, Arduino resets or behaves erratically
Using a non-PWM pin for the signal wire → servo doesn't move or jitters
Not sharing GND between external supply and Arduino → servo moves erratically
Calling myServo.attach() before the power is stable → servo twitches
